data.body:
PASS 1 review — T11 (Data-migration / backfill safety) [overlay]: NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS.

T11 is an overlay that applies only when a plan changes a schema/persisted format or backfills data. This plan adds visual editing (render + ADD/REMOVE/EDIT) for the existing `batch` step `criteria` list, batch params, and the existing `if:`/`when` overlay predicate, with a BPMN<->IR round-trip that "preserves clean git diffs + schema-validated text." It does not introduce a schema migration, a persisted-format change, or a bulk data backfill: the v2 IR JSON Schema and the v1->v2 migrate shim already shipped under separate closed children (b44a), and this plan operates on individual per-workflow IR files at edit time. Per T11's ANTI-FP rule (not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory or non-migrating changes), there are no T11 findings.

Affirmations: no schema change or backfill is introduced; the round-trip invariant is an edit-time lossless-correctness guarantee on single files, not a migration execution path — so T11(a)-(e) (expand-contract, batching, resumability, dual-write window, rollback/data-loss) are out of scope and correctly absent.
